comments.author: @halyna49
comments.text: This is fake . We don’t paint pysanky we write on them with a stylus and use batik method of dying the eggs .  The city was not shown with courtyards .  The Vyshyvka was in neon ugly colors and the old women were dressed in winter Siberian garb in summer time .  The bread was not like bread we make . The Easter basket was not shown only ugly painted eggs .  You showed pilmeny not varenyky. Why do you do this and for whom? You showed a river town not a steppe. You showed a city not mountains or hutsul people. You showed a train not a river.  You showed mountains when talking about rivers .  What a mess !

comments.author: @jaxturner6896
comments.text:
Would I recommend Ukraine for a vacation right now? My answer would be no. There are many beautiful places to visit there, but until the war ends and travel advisories are reduced, it is generally not considered a normal or low-risk tourist destination.
The key issue is that Ukraine is still at war with Russia. Missile and drone attacks can occur throughout the country, including major cities such as Kyiv, Odesa, and Lviv. Air raid alerts, curfews, transportation disruptions, and damage to infrastructure remain realities of daily life in many regions.
